title: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries (annual) — United States, substitute for the Vol. III per-country report (which does not exist for the domestic USA)
source_org: U.S. Geological Survey, National Minerals Information Center
time_coverage: 2022, 2023, 2024 (data years published Jan of following year)
units: metric tons / other units per commodity as described per-table in each file
notes: IMPORTANT STRUCTURAL NOTE: the task asked for the USA equivalent of the Vol. III 'myb3-{year}-{country-slug}.pdf' per-country report pattern used for Iran/Saudi Arabia/Turkey/Russia/Argentina, but confirmed via https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/international-minerals-statistics-and-information that the United States is NOT listed in the international A-Z country index at all (that program covers ~150 countries other than the US) and https://pubs.usgs.gov/myb/vol1/2022/ confirms domestic US mineral data is instead published as USGS Minerals Yearbook VOLUME I ('Metals and Minerals'), organized as ~90 separate single-commodity chapters (e.g. myb1-2022-copper.pdf, myb1-2022-lithium.pdf) rather than one consolidated country report — downloading all ~90 chapters per year was out of scope. Substituted with the annual Mineral Commodity Summaries (MCS), USGS's single flagship yearly document that DOES cover the whole US mineral industry (all ~90 commodities, domestic + world production/reserves context) in one PDF, functionally the closest single-document analog to what Vol. III gives other countries. Downloaded the 3 most recent years (2022-2024) for rough era-comparability with the other countries' 2022/2023 files. All confirmed genuine PDF via `file`, well under the 500MB size cap (each ~12-15MB, larger than other countries' files because MCS covers all commodities at once rather than per-country subset).
